title: Proxy: token forwarding and buyFor / donatePool flows
what_to_reason: Residual token capture: whether leftover balances can be stolen or misrouted
Use of `token.approve(stackAddress, _balance)` — race and allowance issues
Behaviour with non-standard ERC20 tokens (no return values, fee-on-transfer)
Behavior of the fallback (rejects native token transfers)
how_to_reason: Line-by-line review of data flow: incoming tokens -> contract -> approve -> stack contract
Look for checks-effects-interactions and ensure no state is left inconsistent before external calls
Consider token implementations that do not return booleans or charge transfer fees
Enumerate attack scenarios: front-running approvals, sandwiching, and allowance griefing
edge_cases: ERC20 tokens that revert/return false on `approve`/`transferFrom`
Multiple concurrent calls causing unexpected `_balance` accumulation
Stack contract misbehaviour or malicious `stackAddress` implementation
